description: Italian far-left Marxist-Leninist militant group formed in 1970, seeking a revolutionary state through armed struggle. Responsible for numerous violent attacks during Italy's Years of Lead, including the kidnapping and murder of former prime minister Aldo Moro in 1978. The group was broken up in the 1980s but saw a resurgence in the late 1990s to 2000s.

country_links.evidence_quote: Romanian defector Ion Mihai Pacepa claimed that the Red Brigades' primary foreign support came from the Czechoslovak StB...
country_links.context: The Red Brigades received support from the Czechoslovak StB, including weapons and training.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1985 some Italian members living in France returned to Italy. The same year, French President François Mitterrand guaranteed immunity from extradition to BR members living in France...
country_links.context: The Red Brigades had members living in France under immunity from extradition.

country_links.evidence_quote: Italian journalist Loretta Napoleoni said... that he used to sail between Lebanon and Italy during summers, ferrying Soviet weapons for a fee from the PLO to Sardinia...
country_links.context: The Red Brigades ferried weapons from Lebanon to Italy.

country_links.evidence_quote: Smuggled Czechoslovak-made Škorpion submachine pistols were used by the Red Brigades... logistic support and training were carried out directly by the Czechoslovak StB both in Prague and at remote PLO training camps in North Africa and Syria.
country_links.context: The Red Brigades received training at PLO camps in Syria.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 17 December 1981, four members of the BR, posing as plumbers, invaded the Verona apartment of U.S. Army Brigadier General, James L. Dozier...
country_links.context: The Red Brigades kidnapped a U.S. Army general in Italy.

country_links.evidence_quote: According to Pacepa, support for the Red Brigades was a major part of the operations of UDBA, the intelligence service of non-aligned communist Yugoslavia.
country_links.context: The Red Brigades received support from the Yugoslav intelligence service UDBA.
